message:
refactor(runtime): remove Arc<Mutex<StorageHandle>> indirection — StorageHandle is already Send+Clone

The ObservationRuntime wrapped StorageHandle in Arc<Mutex<>> and used
lock_storage_with_profile() for every storage access, introducing
unnecessary lock contention, lock-hold timing metrics, and explicit
drop(guard) boilerplate throughout the runtime loop. StorageHandle is
already internally arc'd and thread-safe (it uses a tokio mpsc channel
to serialize writes), so the outer Mutex served no purpose.

Changes:
- Changed storage field from Arc<Mutex<StorageHandle>> to StorageHandle
- Removed all lock_storage_with_profile() calls and associated
  metrics.record_storage_lock_hold() instrumentation
- Direct method calls on storage: retention_cleanup, checkpoint, vacuum,
  database_page_stats, get_pane, upsert_pane, get_max_seq,
  write_queue_depth, write_queue_capacity, is_writable, record_maintenance
- Simplified db_path access in snapshot engine initialization

benches/mux_client_ops.rs: Improved benchmark accuracy:
- Added approx_list_payload_bytes() helper to estimate PDU payload size
- Changed throughput metric from Elements (window count) to Bytes
  (approximate payload size) for more meaningful PDU decode benchmarks
- Renamed benchmark cases from "small/medium/large" to
  "approx_64B/approx_1KB/approx_64KB" reflecting actual payload sizes
- Adjusted medium case window count 64→16 and large title length 64→112
  to better represent realistic PDU size distribution

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl with current issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
